description_long: This is a tall, narrow ceramic vase with a flared rim and a rounded base, showing significant signs of age and restoration. The surface is matte and uneven, with visible cracks and areas where the vessel has been pieced back together, suggesting it is an ancient or archaeological artifact. The coloration is a muted blend of earthy browns and grays, with some darker patches and lighter weathered areas. Around the upper section, just below the rim, there is a faint incised decorative band with a simple geometric motif. The rim is irregular, with chips and missing fragments, and the body displays a patchwork of repaired breaks. The interior appears hollow and unfinished, consistent with hand-formed pottery.
description: A tall, weathered ceramic vase with a flared rim, visible cracks, and subtle geometric decoration near the top. The surface is matte, earthy, and shows signs of age and restoration.
description_view_0: This view highlights the irregular, chipped rim and the extensive crack lines running vertically down the vase's body.
description_view_1: This side shows the most intact portion of the rim and the clearest section of the incised geometric band below the lip.
description_view_2: This angle reveals a large missing fragment at the rim and a dense network of cracks on the lower body.
description_view_3: This view displays the patchwork of repairs and the uneven coloration, with the rim's damage most prominent.
